data.title: "AP Top Financial News at 12:35 a.m. EDT: Amazon's Stock Soars, Morgan Stanley's Earnings Impress!"
data.description:
Ladies and gentlemen, buckle up! The market is on fire, and we've got the hottest news straight from the AP top financial News at 12:35 a.m. EDT. Let's dive right in!

First up, amazon.com (AMZN) is absolutely crushing it! With a total return of 51.46% over the past 12 months, it's blowing the S&P 500 out of the water. The YTD total return is 28.37%, and that's just the beginning. This stock is a rocket ship, and you don't want to miss the launch!



Now, let's talk about morgan stanley. This financial powerhouse has a market cap of $180.14 billion and an enterprise value of $66.06 billion. The stock price has increased by +28.32% in the last 52 weeks, and the beta is 1.34, indicating higher volatility than the market average. But don't let that scare you—this is a company with a strong track record and a bright future.



Morgan Stanley's trailing PE ratio is 14.05, and the forward PE ratio is 12.99. The PEG ratio is 0.82, which is below 1, indicating that the stock may be undervalued based on its earnings growth potential. The company has a current ratio of 2.09 and a quick ratio of 1.76, showing strong liquidity and the ability to meet short-term obligations. The return on equity (ROE) is 13.17%, suggesting that it is generating a healthy return on its shareholders' investments.

But here's the kicker: Morgan Stanley pays an annual dividend of $3.70, with a dividend yield of 3.31%. This provides investors with a steady income stream. The company has a payout ratio of 46.54%, indicating that it is retaining a significant portion of its earnings for reinvestment and growth.

The next confirmed earnings date for Morgan Stanley is Friday, April 11, 2025, before market open. Mark your calendars, folks! This is a date you won't want to miss. The ex-dividend date is January 31, 2025, so make sure you're in the know if you want to receive the upcoming dividend payment.

Now, let's talk about the broader market. The tech sector is on fire, with giants like Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet (GOOGL) leading the charge. MSFT has a 10-year total return of 1,169.09%, and GOOGL has a 10-year total return of 533.37%. These are not numbers to be ignored. The market is favoring stability, predictability, and consistent growth, and these companies are delivering in spades.

But don't forget about the underdogs. Alibaba Group Holding (BABA) has had a rough ride, with a 3Y total return of -59.54% and a 5Y total return of -50.08%. This is a company that's been through the wringer, but it's also one that could bounce back with the right strategy. Keep an eye on BABA—it might just be the next big thing.

So, what's the takeaway? The market is hot, and there are plenty of opportunities to make some serious cash. Amazon.com is a no-brainer, and Morgan Stanley is a solid bet. The tech sector is booming, and even the underdogs have potential. Stay informed, stay aggressive, and most importantly, stay in the game!

BOO-YAH! This is the time to invest, and you don't want to miss out. The market is your oyster, and these stocks are the pearls. So, get out there and make some money!

data.title: "Fennec Pharmaceuticals: A Deep Dive into Q4 2024 Earnings and the Future of PEDMARK®"
data.description:
Fennec Pharmaceuticals Inc. (NASDAQ: FENC; TSX: FRX) has just reported its financial results for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024, and the numbers are nothing short of impressive. The company achieved a 40% year-over-year increase in PEDMARK® net product sales, reaching $29.6 million for the full year. This growth is a testament to the company's strategic investments and market penetration efforts, particularly in the Adolescent and Young Adult (AYA) segment and academic settings. But what does this mean for the future of fennec pharmaceuticals and its flagship product, PEDMARK®?



The Q4 2024 earnings call, held on March 10, 2025, provided a wealth of information that investors and industry watchers alike should pay close attention to. Let's break down the key takeaways and what they mean for the company's future.

A 40% Year-Over-Year Increase in PEDMARK® Net Product Sales

Fennec Pharmaceuticals reported a 40% year-over-year increase in PEDMARK® net product sales, from $21.3 million in 2023 to $29.6 million in 2024. This growth is particularly notable in the AYA segment and academic settings, where the company has made significant strategic investments to drive awareness of ototoxicity and the adoption of PEDMARK®. The company's efforts to have PEDMARK® added to the NCCN Drug and Biologics Compendium®, a key step in further expanding access and reimbursement pathways, further validates the company's strategic focus on market penetration and adoption.

Early Repayment of Convertible Debt Facility

One of the most significant financial moves made by Fennec Pharmaceuticals in Q4 2024 was the early repayment of $13 million of its convertible debt facility. This strategic action optimizes the company's balance sheet and overall capital structure, effectively saving approximately $1.5 million in future annual interest payments. By reducing its debt burden, Fennec Pharmaceuticals can allocate more resources towards research and development, marketing, and other growth initiatives. This move also eliminates potential dilutive shares, maintaining a stable share structure and potentially attracting more investors who value stability and growth potential.

Commercial Launch of PEDMARQSI® in the United Kingdom and Germany

The commercial launch of PEDMARQSI® in the United Kingdom and Germany is a significant milestone for Fennec Pharmaceuticals. PEDMARQSI® is the first and only approved therapy in the EU and U.K. for the prevention of ototoxicity, or hearing loss, induced by cisplatin chemotherapy in patients aged 1 month to 17 years. This unique position in the market can help Fennec Pharmaceuticals capture a significant share of the pediatric oncology market in these regions. The positive recommendation from the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) in the UK further supports the commercial viability of PEDMARQSI®, driving adoption and reimbursement and contributing to increased revenue streams for Fennec Pharmaceuticals.

Challenges and Opportunities Ahead

While the commercial launch of PEDMARQSI® in the United Kingdom and Germany presents a significant opportunity for Fennec Pharmaceuticals, the company will need to navigate different regulatory environments and reimbursement systems. Additionally, the company will need to build awareness and drive adoption among healthcare providers and patients in these regions, which may require significant investment in marketing and educational initiatives. The potential for competition from other treatments or therapies that may enter the market in the future is also a challenge that the company will need to stay vigilant about.

Financial Performance and Future Outlook

Fennec Pharmaceuticals reported a cash position of $26.6 million as of December 31, 2024, with a $13.4 million increase in cash and cash equivalents between December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2024. The company's financial performance in 2024 was impressive, with revenue increasing by 123.69% compared to the previous year. Losses were -$70,000, -99.56% less than in 2023. The company's strong financial performance and strategic investments position it well for future growth and success.



Conclusion

Fennec Pharmaceuticals' Q4 2024 earnings call provided a wealth of information that investors and industry watchers should pay close attention to. The company's 40% year-over-year increase in PEDMARK® net product sales, early repayment of convertible debt facility, and commercial launch of PEDMARQSI® in the United Kingdom and Germany are all positive indicators of the company's future growth and success. However, the company will need to navigate different regulatory environments and reimbursement systems, build awareness and drive adoption among healthcare providers and patients, and stay competitive in the face of potential future competition. With a strong financial position and strategic investments, Fennec Pharmaceuticals is well-positioned to capitalize on these opportunities and continue to grow and succeed in the biopharmaceutical industry.

data.title: XRP Surges 15% on Speculation of U.S. Treasury Integration
data.description:
Recent discussions have ignited speculation about the potential role of XRP in the U.S. financial system. Joshua Dalton, the founder of TRIBLU, suggested that XRP could become a key asset in the banking sector and U.S. Treasury operations. Dalton's statements have garnered significant attention, particularly his claim that financial institutions may be paving the way for Ripple’s technology to be integrated into the U.S. Treasury and banking system.

Dalton's tweet emphasized that "They are clearing the path for Ripple to become the mainstream coin for US Treasury and banks." He also referenced an earlier post from Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey, who had shared a GIF suggesting that Bitcoin advocate Michael Saylor could eventually embrace XRP. Dalton stated that a rumor from the recent Crypto Summit suggested Saylor was unsettled, experiencing an emotional roller coaster during the event.

These comments have sparked debate within the crypto community, particularly regarding the potential role of XRP in the financial sector. The suggestion that Ripple’s technology could be positioned as a key asset for banks and the U.S. Treasury raises questions about regulatory developments, institutional adoption, and the broader direction of digital assets in the United States. Michael Saylor, known for his strong advocacy of Bitcoin, has consistently positioned BTC as the primary digital asset for institutional use. If the claims from the Crypto Summit hold weight, it could suggest a shift in how financial institutions view XRP compared to Bitcoin.

Dalton's statements highlight a potential shift in the financial landscape, where XRP could play a more significant role. The integration of Ripple’s technology into the U.S. Treasury and banking system would mark a substantial development in the adoption of digital assets by traditional financial institutions. This move could also influence regulatory frameworks, as governments and financial bodies adapt to the growing presence of cryptocurrencies in mainstream finance.

The debate surrounding XRP's potential role in the U.S. financial system underscores the evolving nature of digital assets. As more institutions explore the use of cryptocurrencies, the lines between traditional finance and digital currencies continue to blur. The potential integration of XRP into the U.S. Treasury and banking system could set a precedent for other digital assets, encouraging further adoption and innovation in the financial sector.

The statements made by Dalton and the subsequent discussions at the Crypto Summit have brought attention to the potential of XRP in the financial sector. While the claims remain speculative,

data.title: Crypto Market Plummets: Bitcoin Drops 3%, Ethereum Falls 10% Amid Recession Fears
data.description:
On March 11, the cryptocurrency market experienced significant volatility, with major coins such as Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) facing substantial losses. Bitcoin's price dropped to a multi-month low, losing $80K as of early Asian hours on Tuesday. Ethereum also saw a notable decline, falling by 9% within the same period. This downturn was accompanied by a broader market sentiment of extreme fear, as indicated by the ‘CMC Crypto Fear and Greed Index’ at 15. The market's bearish trend was exacerbated by rising recession fears and increasing trade tensions, which have been a persistent concern since the tariff saga initiated by the previous administration.

The global crypto market cap shrank by nearly 4%, falling to $2.58 trillion. This decline was further underscored by massive liquidations, totaling over $900 million in the past day. The bearish sentiment was not limited to Bitcoin and Ethereum; other major cryptocurrencies such as Solana (SOL) and XRP also experienced significant losses, with SOL and XRP each dropping by 6%. The overall market volatility has sparked concerns among traders and investors, who are grappling with the impact of macroeconomic developments and the potential for a U.S. recession.

Bitcoin's price witnessed a 3% decline, trading at $79,617 as of press time. The coin hit an intraday low of $76,624.25 and a high of $83,955.93. The liquidations recorded over the past day amounted to $314.95 million, according to data. Despite the losses, Bitcoin's market dominance increased by 0.52% to 61.04%, indicating that altcoins were more severely affected by the market downturn.

Ethereum's price fell by 10% in the past 24 hours, closing at $1,846. The coin's intraday low was $1,760.94, and its high was $2,150.71. Ethereum's liquidations totaled $246.20 million over the past 24 hours, and its market dominance stood at 9.1% on Tuesday. XRP's price also declined by nearly 6% in the past

data.title: "Eric Trump's 'Great Time' To Buy ETH Backfires? Ethereum Plunges 30% Since His Buy Call"
data.description:
Ladies and gentlemen, buckle up! We're diving headfirst into the wild world of cryptocurrency, where fortunes can be made and lost in the blink of an eye. Today, we're talking about Ethereum, the second-largest cryptocurrency after Bitcoin, and the rollercoaster ride it's been on since Eric Trump's buy call. Let's get straight to the point: Ethereum has plummeted 30% since Trump's endorsement, and we need to figure out why!



First things first, let's talk about the elephant in the room: volatility. The crypto market is known for its ups and downs, and Ethereum is no exception. Prices can swing wildly, and regulatory uncertainty always looms in the background. Some skeptics argue that Ethereum's price could still face turbulence before making a significant climb. But as the saying goes, "No risk, no reward." Many investors who got into ETH early have seen massive returns, which supports the idea that another big opportunity might be on the horizon.

Now, let's talk about Eric Trump's buy call. On February 3, 2025, he posted on X, stating, “In my opinion, it’s a great time to add $ETH. You can thank me later.” This endorsement sparked excitement and debate among investors, suggesting that Trump sees a strong future for Ethereum. Many investors believe ETH is positioned for growth due to increasing adoption and the rise of DeFi (Decentralized Finance). Ethereum serves as the backbone of many blockchain applications, and upgrades like Ethereum 2.0, which improve speed and reduce fees, have further solidified ETH as a solid long-term play in the eyes of many experts. Institutions are also paying closer attention, and more businesses are integrating ETH into their systems.

But here's the kicker: Trump’s World Liberty (@worldlibertyfi) purchased an additional 1,826 $ETH worth $5M, bringing their total ETH holdings to 61,114 $ETH ($205M) at an average price of $3,354. However, this investment has resulted in a loss of $31M. This significant investment by Trump’s World Liberty, despite the loss, indicates a strong belief in the long-term potential of Ethereum. The transfer of significant amounts of various assets, including 86,296 $ETH, to coinbase Prime further underscores the confidence in Ethereum's future performance.

So, what does this all mean for the broader cryptocurrency market and the adoption of blockchain technology? Ethereum's price volatility can have both positive and negative long-term implications. While it can drive investment and innovation, it can also deter risk-averse investors and impact the development of blockchain applications. Therefore, it is essential for investors and developers to carefully consider the risks and opportunities associated with Ethereum's volatility.

In conclusion, Ethereum's price volatility is a double-edged sword. It can drive investment and innovation, but it can also deter risk-averse investors and impact the development of blockchain applications. Therefore, it is essential for investors and developers to carefully consider the risks and opportunities associated with Ethereum's volatility. So, are you ready to take the plunge, or will you sit on the sidelines and watch the action unfold? The choice is yours, but remember: "No risk, no reward!"

data.title: Watch Out! Wall Street Strategists Are Rushing To Dial Back Their S&P 500 Forecasts!
data.description:
Over the past two years, stock market forecasters have continuously raised their expectations for the S&P 500 index to keep up with the market's relentless rise. However, less than three months into this year, sell-side strategists from firms including jpmorgan and rbc Capital Markets are beginning to express caution about their bullish 2025 forecasts. The reason lies in the concerns over slowing economic growth triggered by U.S. President Trump's tariff policies, which have also led to turbulence in the U.S. stock market.

Although no forecaster has explicitly lowered their expectations yet, this shift in sentiment, coming less than three weeks after the S&P 500 hit a record high, reflects growing uncertainty among Wall Street forecasters. According to an analysis by piper sandler & Co., historically, strategists' consensus targets tend to lag behind the market's actual performance by about 60 days.

JPMorgan strategist Dubravko Lakos-Bujas wrote in a note to clients, "It will remain difficult to fully handicap the potential policy downsides given lack of clarity on timing, scope, and depth of changes. In the interim, investors should embrace volatility."

His team warned last Thursday that their year-end target for the S&P 500 of 6,500 - a 13% increase from last Friday's closing price—might not be achieved by December due to the large standard error around this figure and increasing uncertainty.

Michael Kantrowitz, chief investment strategist at Piper Sandler, said, "I don't think anybody has more conviction today at all: more uncertainty, yes - a wider band of outcomes, yes."

Lakos-Bujas noted that the S&P 500 could fluctuate between his initial year-end target and a low of 5,200 throughout 2025.

This cautious stance contrasts sharply with earlier this year when JPMorgan and other major investment banks predicted steady market gains in the coming months, driven by deregulation, tax cuts, and other Trump policies perceived as growth-friendly.

The once-strong rally even turned Morgan Stanley's Michael Wilson, one of the market's most prominent bears until mid-2024, into a bull. The strategist still expects the S&P 500 to reach 6,500 by the end of 2025.

However, tariffs on goods from trade partners like Canada, Mexico, and China have dampened forecasters' optimism. Since Trump took office, U.S. stocks have faced a tough period, with the benchmark index falling in five out of seven weeks, and the Nasdaq 100 entering correction territory last Friday.

Wilson has turned cautious, warning that U.S. stocks could fall another 5% due to concerns over the impact of tariffs on corporate earnings and reduced fiscal spending. He expects the S&P 500 to drop to around 5,500 in the first half of the year before recovering. "I don't think anybody has more conviction today at all. More uncertainty? Yes. A wider band of outcomes? Yes." Wilson said.

The strategist also warned that the benchmark index could fall 20% if the economy slips into a recession. "We are not there, but things can change quickly so it's useful to know the downside in the bear case to manage one's risk."

JPMorgan's trading desk has also shifted to a tactically bearish stance, citing expectations of a significant decline in GDP, downward earnings revisions, and, most importantly, the need for Wall Street to reassess its year-end S&P 500 targets.

RBC Capital Markets' Lori Calvasina lowered her bear-case target for the S&P 500 from 5,775 to 5,600 based on a new stress-test model that now reflects flat corporate earnings and varying inflation and interest rate scenarios.

Calvasina wrote in a note to clients, "Strong post-election vibes were an important part of the bullish consensus on 2025 for US equities, which has come under pressure." She also asserted that the risk of a 'growth scare'—defined as a 14% to 20% decline - has increased for the S&P 500.

Nevertheless, strategists, including Calvasina, are sticking to their year-end targets for the S&P 500 for now, as the economy remains robust based on the latest jobs data and comments from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Powell said last Friday that the Fed is in no rush to adjust monetary policy. According to a compiled forecast survey, Wall Street on average expects the S&P 500 to end 2025 slightly above 6,500.

data.title: "NET Power Inc. Q4 2024 Earnings Call: Navigating Challenges and Opportunities"
data.description:
The Q4 2024 earnings call for net power Inc. (NPWR) provided a comprehensive overview of the company's performance and strategic initiatives amidst a challenging market environment. The call highlighted several key contradictions and challenges, including CapEx fluctuations, cost reduction strategies, regulatory impacts, and international market priorities. Let's dive into the details and analyze the implications for investors.

Market Challenges and Cost Estimation

One of the most significant revelations from the earnings call was the substantial increase in the total installed costs for Project Permian. The estimated costs, now between $1.7 billion and $2 billion, represent a 100% increase from the previous forecast. This dramatic rise was attributed to market challenges, tight global energy supply chains, and site-specific factors unique to West Texas. These challenges underscore the complexity and unpredictability of cost management in large-scale energy projects.



Technological Advancements and Testing

Despite the cost challenges, NET Power has made significant technological advancements. The company successfully completed the Front-End Engineering and Design (FEED) for Project Permian, with no technical fatal flaws identified. Additionally, the La Porte demo facility has accumulated over 140 fired hours of operation, achieving mission on demand. These milestones are crucial steps towards commercializing clean gas power technology.

Cost Reduction Strategies

To mitigate the cost challenges, NET Power is implementing several strategies. The company has paused long-lead releases for Project Permian and commenced a post-FEED optimization and value engineering exercise. This exercise aims to identify areas where costs can be meaningfully and efficiently reduced. Additionally, NET Power is exploring modular multi-unit feasibility studies for Gulf Coast projects, which are expected to achieve further cost reductions through greater economies of scale, more equipment pre-fabrication, less in-field labor, and lower transportation costs.

Regulatory Environment and Inflationary Pressures

The current regulatory environment and inflationary pressures are significantly impacting NET Power's cost structure and operational strategies. The cost of combined cycle gas turbines (CCGTs) has increased from $1,100 per kW to $2,200 per kW, and this inflation is also impacting NET Power's cost structure. To address these challenges, the company is focusing on strategic cost reductions through multi-plant initiatives and modularization. NET Power ended 2024 with over $530 million in cash, cash equivalents, and investments, providing significant financial resources to optimize its technology and establish strategic partnerships to fund future projects.

International Market Priorities

NET Power is also focusing on international market priorities, particularly in California. The company has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Carbon TerraVault to develop low carbon, reliable power solutions in California. This partnership is expected to expand NET Power's product offering and licensing revenue, further positioning the company for growth.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the Q4 2024 earnings call for NET Power Inc. highlighted both challenges and opportunities. The company is navigating significant cost estimation challenges and inflationary pressures, but it is also making technological advancements and implementing cost reduction strategies. With a strong liquidity position and strategic partnerships, NET Power is well-positioned to commercialize its clean gas power technology and meet the growing demand for clean, firm power solutions. Investors should closely monitor the company's progress in cost reduction and technological advancements, as these factors will be crucial in determining its future success.

data.title: "3 High Growth Tech Stocks To Watch In Asia"
data.description:
LISTEN UP, INVESTORS! The tech sector in Asia is ON FIRE, and you don't want to miss out on these three high-growth stocks that are set to explode. With the region's ICT spending projected to reach a staggering USD $1.4 trillion by 2025, and a CAGR of 5.8% through 2028, the time to act is NOW! Let's dive into the details and see why these stocks are must-haves for your portfolio.



1. AI and Automation Stocks: The Future is Here!

AI and automation are revolutionizing the manufacturing sector in Asia. By 2027, more than 30% of Asia-based Top 2000 manufacturers will invest in new advanced planning & scheduling deployments, leading to perfect order (PO) rates of above 95%. This is a game-changer, folks! Companies like Bosch are already using AI-powered image recognition for quality control, and the results are mind-blowing. Don't miss out on this AI revolution—it's the next big thing in tech!

2. E-commerce and Digital Payment Solutions: The Shopping Spree Continues!

E-commerce is booming in Southeast Asia, with online retail sales expected to account for over 20% of total retail sales by 2025. Platforms like Lazada, Shopee, and Tokopedia are leading the charge, and digital payment solutions like GCash, GrabPay, and dana are making transactions seamless. This is a no-brainer—get in on the action before it's too late!

3. Fintech and Blockchain Technology: The Future of Finance!

The fintech sector is exploding, with digital wallets and blockchain technology becoming ubiquitous. This is the future of finance, and you don't want to be left behind. Companies in this space are offering innovative financial solutions that cater to the unbanked population and provide secure, transparent transactions. This is a must-own sector—don't miss out on the fintech boom!

So, there you have it—three high-growth tech stocks to watch in Asia. The region is poised for explosive growth, and these stocks are at the forefront of the tech revolution. Don't sit on the sidelines—get in on the action and watch your portfolio soar!

data.title: Strategy (MSTR) Drops 15% Amid $21B Preferred Stock Offering
data.description:
Michael Saylor, co-founder and chairman of Strategy, has unveiled an ambitious plan to raise up to $21 billion through the issuance of preferred stock. This move is part of a broader strategy to acquire more Bitcoin (BTC) and bolster the company's holdings in the cryptocurrency market. The new offering will consist of 8% series A perpetual-strike preferred shares, which are convertible into class A common stock. The company plans to sell these shares through an “at the market offering” program, allowing for flexibility in timing and pricing. This approach builds on a previous successful effort in January, when Strategy raised $563 million by issuing preferred shares priced at $80 each, which were offered at a discount to their market value.

Preferred stocks are unique hybrid securities that combine features of both equity and debt, offering investors a fixed dividend while providing a claim on company assets. The favorable terms of the January deal reportedly attracted significant investor interest, contributing to a strong performance of the newly issued shares. Since late October, Strategy has been actively acquiring Bitcoin, and the latest capital raise is part of a broader strategy to secure $42 billion over the next few years through various securities offerings. This includes a focus on selling fixed-income securities while managing common stock sales to fund additional BTC purchases. Currently, the firm holds approximately 499,096 Bitcoin, valued at around $42 billion.

Despite this purchase plan, Strategy reported that it did not purchase any Bitcoin between March 3 and March 9, according to a filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission. This pause comes amid a fluctuating cryptocurrency market. The preferred stock market has seen varied performance; while the shares climbed 18% from their initial pricing, they faced a decline of over 6% in a recent trading session as the supply increased. Despite this fluctuation, the preferred shares have outperformed common stock and Bitcoin over the same period, suggesting a robust demand from investors.

As seen in the daily chart, shares of Strategy (MSTR) experienced a drop of around 15% to $238 on Monday, reflecting broader market trends that have seen the company’s stock down approximately 10% this year. In contrast, shares have surged over 2,200% since Saylor began investing in Bitcoin as an inflation hedge in 2020, while Bitcoin itself has risen over 600%.

data.title: Cardano (ADA) Drops 10% as Large Transactions Decline
data.description:
Cardano (ADA) is currently facing a critical juncture as its price oscillates between $0.80 and $0.66, leaving traders and investors on edge. The cryptocurrency's ability to maintain these support levels will be pivotal in determining its future trajectory. Recent market activity has shown a significant drop in large transactions, which has raised concerns about the interest of major investors and the potential for ADA’s price recovery.

Crypto analyst Ali Martinez has highlighted the importance of holding the $0.80–$0.66 level. A failure to do so could trigger further declines, potentially leading to deeper price consequences. The current price situation has brought ADA back to this support range, which has been crucial in previous market cycles. The volatility in the crypto market has been evident, with ADA witnessing a decline over the past week.

Technical indicators are currently reflecting mixed signals for ADA. The Bollinger Bands show ADA trading near the lower band, hinting at potential oversold conditions. The immediate resistance is marked by the middle band, while the key breakout level remains higher. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is calculated at 47.04, with movements above 50 indicating increasing buying pressure and drops below 40 pointing towards rising selling activity. The MACD has recently shown a bearish crossover, suggesting a possible continuation of the downtrend unless market momentum shifts.

Market dynamics have illustrated significant activity, with a remarkable increase in ADA’s trading volume. However, the Open Interest has dipped, as traders appear to be liquidating positions in the face of uncertainty. Options volume has also plummeted, reflecting lowered speculative engagement. Despite these challenges, there seems to be renewed network interest with active addresses increasing, alongside new addresses climbing and zero-balance addresses growing.

The number of large transactions has seen a significant decline recently, which may illustrate lessened interest from major investors. This decreasing trend in large transactions could potentially hinder any upward movement in ADA’s price. Currently, ADA is trading within the range of $0.80 to $0.90. Its ability to remain above critical support levels will be vital in determining its potential for momentum recovery. A rebound above $0.80 may lead to a movement towards $1.00, while failing to maintain above $0.66 could lead the market into further declines.

data.title: India's Web3 Developer Market Grows 140% in a Decade
data.description:
India has emerged as a significant contributor to the global Web3 ecosystem, despite the absence of locally tailored crypto regulations. The country's share of global Web3 developers has grown from 5% to 12% over the past decade, positioning it as the second-largest developer market globally, trailing only the United States. This growth is driven by several key factors, including retail crypto transactions on centralized services, institutional adoption, and retail DeFi transactions.

The Indian Web3 landscape is dominated by a younger generation, with approximately 80% of all blockchain developers in the country falling between the ages of 18 and 27. These developers are particularly drawn to blockchains like Solana, Ton, Aptos, and Base, which are gaining traction across various sectors due to the expanding presence of layer-1 and layer-2 ecosystems. However, challenges remain, such as the high cost of customer acquisition for Web3 gaming projects and the lack of quality gameplay beyond financial incentives.

Despite these hurdles, investments into the Indian Web3 landscape saw a 224% increase in 2024 compared to the previous year. This growth is attributed to various funding avenues, including local funds, ecosystem funds, and corporate venture arms of leading exchanges. The report highlights that the lack of traditional venture capital and the absence of growth capital in the Web3 world make it difficult for Indian firms to raise funds, leading entrepreneurs to explore crowd sales as an alternative.

India is home to the second-largest developer market and the third-largest founder base globally, making it a hub for innovation in the Web3 space. However, barriers to large-scale investments include slower-than-anticipated growth of startups and unclear regulations and compliances. The report calls for reforms in crypto tax deductions and reporting, as well as federal guidance and tax implications to incentivize the growth of the Web3 sector.

Despite an active high-tax environment on cryptocurrency, small-scale crypto investments have seen an uptick in India. Traders generally prefer small, frequent trades, with a significant portion of the market being female, highlighting the potential for greater participation. The policy wish list for the Indian Web3 sector includes a regulatory framework for virtual asset service providers, tax rationalization, streamlined banking and payment access, and clarity on existing regulations.

Recent regulatory initiatives, such as the blocking of URLs of locally unlicensed crypto exchanges

data.title: Dogecoin Drops 30% as Traders Flock to Remittix RTX
data.description:
In the current crypto market, the meme coin sector is experiencing a downturn, largely due to the impact of the newly established Strategy Bitcoin reserve by the Trump administration. This reserve, which focuses on holding Bitcoin as a hedge against inflation, has led to a significant price drop in Dogecoin, which has historically followed Bitcoin's price movements. The clarification that the reserve will only hold seized Bitcoin, rather than purchasing new tokens, has resulted in a 30% loss for Dogecoin over the past week.

As the Dogecoin price continues to decline, many traders are turning their attention to a new DeFi project called Remittix. This project offers a unique use case that has captured the interest of crypto traders, with its potential return on investment (ROI) being a significant draw. The bearish situation surrounding Dogecoin has led smart meme traders to invest heavily in the Remittix project, seeking to capitalize on its potential for growth.

Remittix aims to revolutionize the way crypto users transfer their assets into fiat currencies. The project provides a seamless and secure method for liquidating crypto assets into a wide range of popularly traded fiat currencies, allowing users to make payments for goods and services directly from their crypto wallets. This feature is particularly appealing to users who want to avoid the complexities and barriers associated with traditional banking systems.

One of the standout features of Remittix is its emphasis on privacy and security. Transactions made through the Remittix network cannot be traced back to the platform, ensuring that users can make bank deposits from their crypto wallets without revealing the origin of the funds. Additionally, the entire setup is built on the Ethereum blockchain, one of the most secure and widely used ecosystems for payment networks.

Remittix is designed to cater to the average crypto user, rather than large corporations and banks. This user-centric approach is expected to drive significant user traffic to the platform, potentially leading to substantial gains for RTX tokens in the long run. The presale for RTX tokens has already raised over $13.8 million, indicating strong demand and investor confidence in the project.

With the current bearish trend in the meme coin sector and the potential for significant gains in the Remittix project, many Dogecoin traders are abandoning their positions in favor of investing in RTX tokens. The declining price of Dogecoin, which has reached four

data.title: Buy The Dip! Tesla & Nasdaq Poised for a Comeback
data.description:
U.S. stocks have sharply deviated from their previous slow bull trend, suffering consecutive declines. Yesterday, the market experienced a "Black Monday", with tesla plunging 15%, marking its worst single-day drop since the 2020 pandemic crash. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq Index plummeted 4%.

Trump's Comments Trigger Market Sell-Off

The immediate catalyst behind the sell-off was Donald Trump's remarks regarding the U.S. economy. In a recent interview, Trump did not rule out the possibility of a recession. When asked whether his tariff policies could drive up inflation, he bluntly stated:

Tariffs could rise over time. They might go up. I don't know if that's predictable.

Recession fears and rising tariffs are two of the biggest concerns for market bulls—both of which were directly triggered by Trump's comments.

Bear Market or Buying Opportunity?

With stocks in free fall, investors are debating whether this is the start of a bear market or a rare buy-the-dip opportunity. The U.S. economic outlook remains uncertain, but history provides valuable insights into how markets tend to react after significant drawdowns.

Tesla: Oversold and Historically Resilient

Tesla has been one of the hardest-hit stocks in this sell-off. The stock's 14-day RSI (Relative Strength Index) has dropped to around 20, signaling extreme oversold conditions.

Historically, there have been five instances where Tesla's RSI fell below 22 (including the current one). In all previous cases, Tesla delivered positive returns over the following 1-month, 6-month, and 1-year periods. While history does not repeat exactly, it often rhymes.

Nasdaq: Strong Rebound Potential

Another major casualty of the sell-off is the Nasdaq Index. The 14-day RSI for the Nasdaq-100 has dropped below 30, also signaling an oversold market.

Looking at historical data, every time the Nasdaq-100 RSI fell below 29, the index had a greater than 50% probability of delivering positive returns over the next week to one year. Moreover, the longer the holding period, the higher the expected returns.

data.title: Oracle Misses Q3 Estimates as Cloud Growth Slows, Stock Under Pressure
data.description:
Oracle Corporation (NYSE: ORCL) reported its fiscal third-quarter earnings after the market close on March 10, 2025, delivering results that came in slightly below analyst expectations on both the top and bottom lines. The company posted adjusted earnings per share of $1.47, just shy of the anticipated $1.49, while revenue came in at $14.13 billion, missing the consensus estimate of $14.4 billion. Despite a 6.4% year-over-year revenue increase, oracle faced a challenging quarter, with its growth metrics not quite meeting market expectations.

One of the key focal points for investors was Oracle’s cloud infrastructure performance, which remains the primary driver of its long-term growth strategy. Cloud revenue, including Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) and Software-as-a-Service (SaaS), reached $6.2 billion, missing the $6.3 billion estimate. In constant currency, cloud revenue grew 25%, slightly below the expected 26.5%. Meanwhile, the IaaS segment, a crucial component of Oracle’s AI-driven growth narrative, generated $2.7 billion in revenue, also falling short of the $2.71 billion forecast. On a constant currency basis, IaaS growth came in at 51%, lower than the expected 54.2%, raising some concerns about whether the company’s AI and cloud momentum remains strong enough to counteract competitive pressures.

Oracle’s remaining performance obligations (RPO), which serve as an indicator of future revenue growth, showed robust expansion. Total RPO reached $97 billion, reflecting a 49% year-over-year increase in U.S. dollars and a 50% gain in constant currency. This was a bright spot for the company, reinforcing expectations for sustained long-term demand, particularly in cloud services. However, investors had been looking for RPO growth to surpass the $6.2 billion street estimate, which added to concerns about the pace of Oracle’s near-term revenue expansion.

Despite these challenges, Oracle maintained solid profitability. Adjusted operating income rose 7% year-over-year to $6.2 billion, only slightly below the $6.25 billion estimate. The adjusted operating margin remained steady at 44%, in line with the prior year and ahead of the expected 43.6%. This suggests that while revenue came in softer than expected, Oracle was able to sustain its cost efficiency, which remains a key factor in driving shareholder value.

Currency fluctuations had a mixed impact on Oracle’s performance this quarter. The company previously faced headwinds from a strong U.S. dollar, which negatively affected revenue growth due to its significant international exposure. However, the dollar’s decline by 6% since mid-January provided some relief, contributing to an 8% year-over-year increase in revenue on a constant currency basis. That said, this figure still fell short of the anticipated 9.09% growth, signaling that foreign exchange benefits were not enough to offset broader growth challenges.

Looking ahead, Oracle’s ability to capitalize on AI-driven revenue remains a crucial theme for investors. The company’s participation in the $100 billion Stargate AI initiative, alongside key industry players like SoftBank and OpenAI, is expected to provide long-term tailwinds. However, competition from Microsoft, Amazon, and emerging AI firms like DeepSeek remains an overhang, particularly as Oracle seeks to expand its cloud infrastructure footprint. Analysts will be closely monitoring Oracle’s ability to maintain IaaS growth above 50%, as any sustained deceleration could impact the stock’s valuation.

In response to the earnings report, Oracle shares saw an initial dip in after-hours trading, reflecting investor disappointment over the slight revenue and EPS misses. The stock, already down 7% in the past week and 20% from its November peak, faces continued pressure as broader market concerns—including technology sector weakness and tariff-related disruptions—persist. With shares now trading below their 200-day moving average, Oracle will need a strong Q4 performance and clearer AI revenue visibility to regain investor confidence and reverse its recent downtrend. Investors will be particularly focused on guidance for cloud infrastructure growth and any updates on large AI-driven contract wins in the coming quarters.

data.title: Binance Integrates AVA on Solana Network, Enhances Trading Flexibility
data.description:
On March 11, 2025, Binance announced a significant development in its platform capabilities by integrating ava (AVA) on the Solana network. This integration enables users to deposit and withdraw AVA tokens seamlessly, marking a strategic expansion for the cryptocurrency exchange. The move is part of Binance's ongoing efforts to diversify its offerings and enhance the flexibility and convenience for traders and investors.

The integration of AVA on the Solana network is a notable advancement for both Binance and the broader cryptocurrency ecosystem. Solana, recognized for its high-speed and low-cost transactions, has garnered attention from developers and users. By incorporating AVA, Binance is responding to the increasing demand for decentralized finance (DeFi) solutions and interoperability between different blockchain networks. This integration allows users to leverage the strengths of both Solana and AVA, providing a more robust and versatile trading environment.

This development is anticipated to benefit both the AVA and Solana communities. For AVA holders, the ability to deposit and withdraw tokens on Binance offers a secure and reliable platform for trading and storing their assets. For Solana, the integration with Binance further cements its status as a leading blockchain network, attracting more users and developers to its ecosystem. This collaboration is expected to drive innovation and growth within the cryptocurrency space, as both communities can now interact more seamlessly.

Binance's commitment to innovation is evident in this integration, as the exchange continues to pioneer new developments in the cryptocurrency industry. By expanding its services and integrating new tokens, Binance aims to provide a comprehensive and user-friendly platform for cryptocurrency trading and investment. This move is just one of many steps Binance is taking to maintain its leadership position in the rapidly evolving cryptocurrency landscape, ensuring that it remains at the forefront of technological advancements and market trends.

data.title: "Solar Dominates US Power Additions in 2024, Report Reveals"
data.description:
In a groundbreaking development for the renewable energy sector, solar power accounted for an astonishing 84% of all new electricity-generating capacity added to the U.S. grid in 2024. This remarkable figure underscores the rapid growth and increasing dominance of solar energy in the nation's energy mix. The data, released in the Summer 2024 Quarterly Solar Industry Update by the National Renewable Energy laboratory (NREL), highlights the transformative impact of government policies, technological advancements, and market dynamics on the solar industry.

A Surge in Solar Installations

The U.S. solar market has seen unprecedented growth in 2024, with the installation of 9.4 GWdc of capacity in the second quarter alone. This represents a 29% increase from the same period in 2023, setting a new record for the industry. The utility-scale segment, in particular, has been a significant driver of this growth, with 7.6 GWdc installed in the second quarter, a 59% increase year-over-year. This robust growth is a testament to the increasing demand for renewable energy and the effectiveness of government incentives.



Government Policies and Incentives

One of the key factors driving this surge in solar installations is the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), passed in 2022. Since its passage, more than 85 GW of manufacturing capacity have been added across the solar supply chain, including nearly 35 GW of new module capacity. This has significantly boosted domestic manufacturing and procurement, making solar energy more accessible and affordable. For instance, domestic module manufacturing capacity increased by over 10 GW to 31.3 GW in the second quarter of 2024 as more facilities continued to come online.

Technological Advancements and Cost Reductions

Technological advancements and cost reductions have also played a crucial role in the growth of the solar industry. The average global factory gate module price dropped another 10% in the third quarter of 2024, reaching $0.10/Watt direct current (Wdc), with some module prices falling below production costs. This price reduction has made solar energy more competitive with traditional energy sources. Additionally, global polysilicon spot prices fell 10% from early May to late July 2024, further contributing to cost reductions.

State-Level Initiatives

State-level initiatives have also been instrumental in driving solar growth. Texas and Florida continue to be the top-ranked states for capacity installed in 2024, with 7.9 GWdc and 3.1 GWdc online through the third quarter, respectively. These states have implemented policies and initiatives that support solar energy development, contributing to the overall increase in installations.

Challenges and Opportunities

While the solar industry has seen remarkable growth, it also faces several challenges. The utility-scale segment, which is the largest segment, continues to be limited by a lack of labor availability, high-voltage equipment constraints, and interconnection delays. These factors are expected to constrain growth in the next five years, despite high demand for solar. Additionally, high interest rates across the country have dampened demand, particularly in the residential solar market.

However, the opportunities presented by government support, increasing demand, and technological advancements make the solar industry an attractive sector for investment. The U.S. solar industry is expected to install at least 40 GWdc per year from 2025 onward, with the utility-scale segment continuing to be the largest contributor to this growth.

Implications for the Energy Mix

The increasing share of solar energy in the U.S. energy mix has significant implications for the overall energy landscape. Solar energy is becoming the primary driver of renewable energy expansion in the U.S., with other renewable energy sources such as wind, hydro, and geothermal not showing similar levels of growth. This trend is crucial for reducing carbon emissions and mitigating climate change. The materials mention that "Carbon Emissions Reduced: 224 million metric tons," highlighting the environmental benefits of solar energy adoption.

Future Investment Decisions

Investors in the solar industry will need to navigate the current challenges and uncertainties to make informed investment decisions. While the long-term outlook for the solar industry remains positive, investors will need to consider investing in labor training programs and high-voltage equipment to address the current constraints in the utility-scale segment. Additionally, given the potential tariffs on imports from Southeast Asian countries, investors may look to diversify their supply chain by investing in domestic manufacturing or sourcing from other countries not subject to the proposed tariffs.

In conclusion, the 84% share of new power added by solar in 2024 reflects its dominant position among renewable energy sources. This trend has significant implications for the overall energy mix, promoting a cleaner and more sustainable energy future for the U.S. While the solar industry faces several challenges, the opportunities presented by government support, increasing demand, and technological advancements make it an attractive sector for investment. However, investors will need to navigate the current challenges and uncertainties to make informed investment decisions.

data.title: "US Private Funds Ask SEC to Rethink Gensler-Era Rules"
data.description:
Ladies and gentlemen, buckle up! The private funds industry is in for a wild ride as it asks the SEC to rethink the Gensler-era rules. The landscape has shifted dramatically, and it's time to take a hard look at what's changed and what's next.



The Gensler era brought a tsunami of regulatory changes, aiming to impose a retail-like disclosure and restrictions framework on private funds. The Private Fund Adviser Rules (PFAR) were a cornerstone of this push, but the Fifth Circuit's decision to vacate them in June 2024 was a game-changer. The court's rebuke of the SEC's statutory authority sent shockwaves through the industry, stalling many live proposals and largely halting new rulemaking.

The SEC's enforcement actions in 2024 were relentless, with 583 enforcement actions and $8.2 billion in financial remedies. But with the departure of SEC Enforcement Director Gurbir Grewal and Acting Director Sanjay Wadhwa, the stage is set for a meaningful shift in enforcement strategy in 2025. The new leadership under Paul Atkins is expected to focus on more egregious misconduct, particularly those involving retail investor harm, rather than pursuing technical violations or novel cases.

The climate disclosure rule, finalized in March 2024, is another contentious issue. The rule requires public companies to divulge a host of climate-related information, but it faced nine lawsuits and was voluntarily halted by the SEC pending court rulings. The 8th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in St. Louis agreed to hear the lawsuits on a consolidated basis, adding another layer of uncertainty to the regulatory landscape.

The SEC's Division of Examinations has identified the review of investment advisers to private funds as a key priority. But under Atkins, there may be a greater emphasis on compliance examinations that include a substantive review of firms' implementation efforts, marketing materials, client disclosures, and books and records, rather than aggressive enforcement actions.

The nomination of Paul Atkins as the new SEC Chair is expected to bring a more measured approach to regulation and enforcement. The SEC under Atkins is likely to be less inclined to pursue technical violations by private fund managers or aggressively pursue novel cases. This is because of significant court decisions in 2024 that curbed much of the SEC’s private fund rulemaking agenda under Chairman Gary Gensler, and the change in presidential administration, bringing with it a shift in priorities and a change in enforcement approach.

The expectation is that the SEC staff under SEC Chair-nominee Paul Atkins will be less aggressive compared to the staff under his predecessor and instead focused on instances of more egregious misconduct, particularly those involving retail investor harm.



So, what does this mean for private fund managers and investors? The regulatory landscape is in flux, and it's crucial to stay informed and adaptable. The SEC's recent enforcement actions and rulemaking halts have the potential to shape the future of private fund management by influencing regulatory compliance, investor confidence, and the approach to ESG factors. The ongoing legal challenges and leadership changes at the SEC add layers of complexity to this evolving landscape.

In summary, the private funds industry is at a crossroads. The Gensler-era rules have been challenged, and the SEC's enforcement strategy is shifting. It's time for private fund managers to take a hard look at their compliance efforts and for investors to stay vigilant. The future of private fund management is uncertain, but one thing is clear: the industry is in for a wild ride.

data.title: Tesla and Microstrategy Lead Trading Volume Surge Amid Market Volatility
data.description:
1. Tesla (Nasdaq: TSLA)
Tesla plunged by -15.43%, with the trading volume of 43.78B. Former President Donald Trump announced plans to purchase a tesla vehicle to demonstrate support for Elon Musk, amid claims of illegal resistance against Tesla aimed at harming Musk.

2. Nvidia (Nasdaq: NVDA)
Nvidia dropped solidly by -5.07%, with the trading volume of 39.46B. Nvidia is expected to announce the new GB300 AI chip at its annual GTC conference in March, integrating water cooling technology to address cooling demands. This innovation may accelerate the adoption of water-cooling systems in the market.

3. Apple (Nasdaq: AAPL)
Apple dropped solidly by -4.85%, with the trading volume of 16.40B. Apple revealed a lightweight iPhone 17 Air design, added F1 and UEFA women's soccer support to its sports app, and plans to launch redesigned iOS 19 and macOS 16 systems. EU may fine Apple under digital market laws, and Siri AI upgrade postponed.

4. Meta Platforms (Nasdaq: META)
Meta Platforms dropped solidly by -4.42%, with the trading volume of 13.13B. Meta collaborates with Ray-Ban to launch the limited edition "Ray-Ban Meta x Coperni" smart glasses for Paris Fashion Week, featuring a transparent black frame and priced at $549, with 3,600 units available.

5. Microsoft (Nasdaq: MSFT)
Microsoft dropped solidly by -3.34%, with the trading volume of 12.52B. Microsoft plans to discontinue the "Microsoft Remote Desktop" app in 2025, is developing a new generation Xbox console for 2027, and encourages new Windows 11 installations for security enhancement. CEO Satya Nadella emphasizes system integration and product development over large-scale model creation.

6. Amazon (Nasdaq: AMZN)
Amazon.Com dipped mildly by -2.36%, with the trading volume of 12.07B. Amazon Web Services launched Ocelot, a new quantum computing chip, aiming to reduce quantum error correction costs by 90%. Also, Amazon Web Services introduced the fully managed DeepSeek-R1 model on Amazon Bedrock, allowing scalable deployment of production-grade generative AI without infrastructure management.

7. Palantir Technologies (NYSE: PLTR)
Palantir Technologies plunged by -10.05%, with the trading volume of 10.65B. Joule Financial's Quint Tatro discussed trading strategies for AI stocks amid market volatility, focusing on Nvidia and Palantir, without specific guidance on stock movements or volume rankings.

8. Broadcom (Nasdaq: AVGO)
Broadcom dropped solidly by -5.39%, with the trading volume of 8.15B. Broadcom's Q1 FY25 earnings call revealed a revenue of $149 billion, a 25% increase, and adjusted EBITDA of $101 billion, a 41% increase, driven by AI semiconductor growth and expanding partnerships.

9. Microstrategy Incorporated (Nasdaq: MSTR)
Microstrategy Incorporated plunged by -16.68%, with the trading volume of 7.75B. Microstrategy acquired significant funding amounting to $77.50 billion on March 10, reflecting robust investor interest in the company's business strategy moving forward.

10. Alphabet (Nasdaq: GOOGL)
Alphabet dropped solidly by -4.49%, with the trading volume of 7.24B. Eric Schmidt, former CEO of Google, joined Relativity Space as CEO, marking his return to a CEO role since he stepped down from Google in 2011. Meanwhile, Google's lucrative search agreement with Apple might be under threat due to antitrust measures.

data.title: Global Markets Plunge 10% Amid Recession Fears, Policy Uncertainty
data.description:
The recent downturn in global financial markets, characterized by significant declines in both stock and cryptocurrency prices, can be attributed to a sudden shift in risk appetite. This shift has seen investors transition from a state of extreme greed to extreme fear within a matter of days, primarily driven by growing concerns over a potential U.S. economic recession.

Leading cryptocurrencies have mirrored the stock market rout, with investors becoming increasingly jittery over recession concerns. The U.S. bond market is signaling a risk-off environment, leading to elevated selling pressure in the equity market and other asset classes, including crypto. The U.S. Treasury Secretary has also stated that as the U.S. economy shifts from public spending to more private spending, an economic slowdown may occur.

The market downturn is not solely due to standard economic factors but is largely influenced by rising uncertainty about U.S. policy. Businesses are becoming increasingly concerned about the potential impact of tariff policies on the economy, which could tip the U.S. into a recession. This uncertainty has led to a deepening sell-off on Wall Street, as investors lose confidence in the market.

Bank stocks have also been hit hard, with recession fears and other economic headwinds causing investors to lose confidence. The expectation that the Federal Reserve will have to cut interest rates in response to a potential recession has driven bond prices higher, further exacerbating the sell-off in the equity market.

Institutional capital has been exiting tech stocks before the downturn. Moving into 2025, hedge fund holdings of the seven tech stocks (Magnificent 7) will drop to the lowest level in 22 months. This shift in risk appetite has led to a historic high in the outflow of funds, resulting in the "flash crash" witnessed in the market.

In the last week of February, outflows from cryptocurrency funds hit a record $26 billion for the week. This was about $5 billion higher than the record set in 2024. The rapid change in sentiment has led to a significant outflow of funds, contributing to the market downturn.

In summary, the current decline in the stock market and cryptocurrency prices is largely due to a shift in risk appetite, as investors become increasingly concerned about a potential U.S. economic recession. This shift is driven by uncertainty about U.S. policy and the potential impact of tariff policies
